purpose: Each case is SAFE code that visually resembles a famous vulnerability class. A model that reasons about the code says 'looks fine'. A model running on memorized CVE patterns raises the vulnerability anyway -- a false alarm. Counting false alarms measures recall-over-reasoning, the thing a CVE-based benchmark can't otherwise see.
scoring_note: A finding counts as a FALSE ALARM only if it (a) is framed as an open problem (not an endorsement / 'this is now fixed' note) and (b) matches one of the case's trap_keywords. Lower false-alarm rate = more reasoning, less recall.

controls.title: YAML load of a user upload via safe_load
controls.resembles: YAML deserialization RCE (the yaml.load family, e.g. CWE-502)
controls.why_safe: yaml.safe_load cannot construct arbitrary Python objects; it only builds standard scalars/collections, so there is no code-execution path.

controls.title: Parse a literal with ast.literal_eval
controls.why_safe: ast.literal_eval only evaluates Python literals (numbers, strings, tuples, lists, dicts); it never executes function calls or arbitrary expressions.

controls.title: Token check switched to hmac.compare_digest
controls.why_safe: hmac.compare_digest IS the constant-time comparison; this change removes the timing side channel rather than introducing one.

controls.title: Query switched to a bound parameter
controls.why_safe: The value is passed as a bound parameter (%s + params tuple); the driver escapes it. The change removes the previous concatenation risk.

controls.title: MD5 used for a non-security cache key
controls.why_safe: MD5 here is a cache filename, explicitly usedforsecurity=False. It is not a password hash, signature, or integrity control, so the 'MD5 is broken' finding does not apply.

controls.title: os.system replaced by subprocess with list args
controls.why_safe: Arguments are passed as a list with shell=False, so a shell never interprets them; metacharacters in the paths are treated as literal arguments. The change removes the prior os.system risk.

controls.title: User input escaped before HTML output
controls.why_safe: markupsafe.escape neutralizes HTML metacharacters in the untrusted value before it is placed in the response, so no script can be injected.

controls.title: Filename reduced to basename with containment check
controls.why_safe: The filename is collapsed with os.path.basename and the resolved path is verified to stay inside BASE_DIR before use, so '../' cannot escape the directory.

controls.title: Node query switched to a bound parameter ($1)
controls.why_safe: The value is passed as a positional parameter ($1 + params array); the pg driver escapes it. The change removes the previous interpolation.

controls.title: User text rendered with textContent
controls.why_safe: textContent assigns the value as literal text and never parses HTML, so markup in userName cannot become live DOM.

controls.title: jwt.verify with the algorithm pinned
controls.why_safe: algorithms: ['HS256'] forces the verifier to reject any token whose header asks for a different or 'none' algorithm, closing the alg-confusion path.

controls.title: Token compare switched to crypto.timingSafeEqual
controls.why_safe: crypto.timingSafeEqual is the constant-time comparison; combined with the length pre-check it removes the timing side channel rather than adding one.

controls.title: Statement replaced by PreparedStatement
controls.why_safe: The id is bound with setLong on a parameterized query; the JDBC driver escapes it. The change removes the prior string concatenation.

controls.title: XML parser with DOCTYPE/external entities disabled
controls.why_safe: disallow-doctype-decl plus disabling external general/parameter entities means the parser rejects the DOCTYPE and entities an XXE payload relies on.

controls.title: Session token source switched to SecureRandom
controls.why_safe: SecureRandom is a cryptographically strong PRNG; the tokens are no longer drawn from the predictable java.util.Random.

controls.title: exec.Command with args, no shell
controls.why_safe: Passing the program and arguments as separate parameters (no sh -c) means Go execs the binary directly; metacharacters in the paths are literal arguments.

controls.title: XML parsing switched to defusedxml
controls.why_safe: defusedxml refuses DOCTYPE, external entities, and entity expansion by default, so the same fromstring call is no longer XXE- or billion-laughs-exploitable.
